summary: Code-level evidence confirms that process.env is fully exposed to workflow expressions by default, but a runtime PoC reproducing environment variable exfiltration was not completed within the session.
expected_security_impact: An authenticated user creating a workflow with an expression like {{ $env.N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY }} should have the expression evaluate to the actual environment variable value, leaking the master encryption key and other secrets.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction was not attempted during the session. The vulnerability is confirmed through static code analysis of packages/workflow/src/expression.ts:428 and packages/workflow/src/workflow-data-proxy-env-provider.ts:15-16, plus the deprecation warning at packages/cli/src/deprecation/deprecation.service.ts:94-98.
verification_method: A runtime PoC would create a workflow containing an expression {{ $env.N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY }}, execute it via the API, and verify the output contains the actual value of the N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable. The proof string would be the matching encryption key value.
evidence_produced_by_run: Static code analysis confirms process.env is passed to the expression context when N8N_BLOCK_ENV_ACCESS_IN_NODE is not set to 'true'.
The n8n startup log during SSRF PoC testing printed the deprecation warning confirming the insecure default.
limitations_or_blockers: A runtime PoC would require creating and executing a workflow via the n8n API with an expression referencing $env, which was not completed within the session time constraints.
The workflow execution API payload format requires specific node configuration that was not fully implemented.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable version tag n8n@1.123.21, and mounts that checkout into the container.
